2604.20135.title: AFMRL: Attribute-Enhanced Fine-Grained Multi-Modal Representation Learning in E-commerce
2604.20135.paper_summary: Multimodal representation is crucial for E-commerce tasks such as identical product retrieval. Large representation models (e.g., VLM2Vec) demonstrate strong multimodal understanding capabilities, yet they struggle with fine-grained semantic comprehension, which is essential for distinguishing highly similar items. To address this, we propose Attribute-Enhanced Fine-Grained Multi-Modal Representation Learning (AFMRL), which defines product fine-grained understanding as an attribute generation task. It leverages the generative power of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to extract key attributes from product images and text, and enhances representation learning through a two-stage training framework: 1) Attribute-Guided Contrastive Learning (AGCL), where the key attributes generated by the MLLM are used in the image-text contrastive learning training process to identify hard samples and filter out noisy false negatives. 2) Retrieval-aware Attribute Reinforcement (RAR), where the improved retrieval performance of the representation model post-attribute integration serves as a reward signal to enhance MLLM's attribute generation during multimodal fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on large-scale E-commerce datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple downstream retrieval tasks, validating the effectiveness of harnessing generative models to advance fine-grained representation learning.
2604.20135.paper_summary_zh: 多模態表示對於電子商務任務（例如相同產品檢索）至關重要。大型表示模型（例如 VLM2Vec）展現出強大的多模態理解能力，但在細粒度語義理解方面存在困難，而這對於區分高度相似的項目是必不可少的。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了增強屬性的細粒度多模態表示學習（AFMRL），將產品的細粒度理解定義為一個屬性生成任務。它利用多模態大型語言模型（MLLM）的生成能力，從產品圖像和文本中提取關鍵屬性，並通過兩階段訓練框架增強表示學習：1）屬性引導對比學習（AGCL），在此過程中，MLLM生成的關鍵屬性用於圖像-文本對比學習訓練過程，以識別困難樣本並過濾掉噪聲假陰性。2）檢索感知屬性強化（RAR），在此過程中，屬性整合後表示模型的檢索性能提升作為獎勵信號，以增強MLLM在多模態細調過程中的屬性生成。對大型電子商務數據集的廣泛實驗表明，我們的方法在多個下游檢索任務中達到了最先進的性能，驗證了利用生成模型推進細粒度表示學習的有效性。
2604.20135.author: Biao Zhang et.al.
2604.20135.authors: Biao Zhang, Lixin Chen, Bin Zhang, Zongwei Wang, Tong Liu, Bo Zheng
